title: MessageSubscription.close() may raise during cleanup if subscription already errored
description:
File: python/src/lichen/client/lci.py, lines 88-90

The MessageSubscription.close() method delegates directly to the underlying subscription:

```python
async def close(self) -> None:
    '''Cancel the inbox Observe relationship.'''
    await self._subscription.close()
```

Issue: If the underlying subscription is in an error state (e.g., aiocoap observation failed), calling close() may raise an exception. The caller, trying to clean up after an error, gets a second exception.

Example scenario:
```python
subscription = await client.observe_inbox()
try:
    async for messages in subscription.messages():
        ...  # Network error here raises LciClientError
except LciClientError:
    await subscription.close()  # This may also raise!
```

Impact: Error handling code may fail unexpectedly, complicating cleanup.

Fix: Make close() idempotent and exception-safe:
```python
async def close(self) -> None:
    try:
        await self._subscription.close()
    except Exception:
        pass  # Already closed or errored
```
close_reason: Fixed: Made MessageSubscription.close() and RawRxSubscription.close() idempotent and exception-safe using contextlib.suppress(Exception)
